channel.title: Context Engineering Daily - Research Papers
channel.description: Latest Research Papers news in Context Engineering

item.title: MentalMARBERT: Domain-Adaptive Pre-training and Two-Stage Fine-Tuning for Arabic Mental Health Disorders Detection
item.description: arXiv:2606.12649v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Detecting mental health disorders from Arabic social media text remains challenging due to dialectal variation, informal language, limited high-quality annotated resources, and severe class imbalance. While English mental health natural language proce...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> study, arxiv, framework, model, fine-tuning | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: AfriSUD: A Dependency Treebank Collection for Evaluating Models on African Languages
item.description: arXiv:2606.12708v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Despite their linguistic diversity and global significance, African languages remain underrepresented in research and resources to support NLP. We aim to bridge this gap by introducing AfriSUD, the first large-scale collection of syntactically annotat...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> transformer, research, framework, LLM, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Does AI Reviewer See the Full Picture? Attacking and Defending Multimodal Peer Review
item.description: arXiv:2606.12716v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The integration of Large Language Models (LLMs) and Multimodal LLMs (MLLMs) into scientific peer-review workflows introduces novel and significant risks for adversarial manipulation, especially given the multimodal nature of scientific papers where fi...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> multimodal, framework, large language model, LLM, cross-modal | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Deployment-Centered Evaluation: Predicting Query-Level Rejection Risk in a Clinical LLM System
item.description: arXiv:2606.12702v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large language models (LLMs) are increasingly integrated into clinical systems, making it essential to evaluate the real-world utility of these systems. However, static benchmarks tend to measure correctness rather than user acceptance, aggregate perf...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> study, analysis, large language model, LLM, context | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Restless bandits with imperfect binary feedback: PCL-indexability analysis and computation
item.description: arXiv:2606.11192v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We study restless bandits with binary latent states and imperfect binary feedback, motivated by opportunistic spectrum access with sensing errors. For the associated belief-state model, we develop a partial conservation laws (PCL)-based analytical and...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> study, analysis, product, framework, paper | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Few-Shot Resampling for Scalable Statistically-Sound Data Mining
item.description: arXiv:2606.11235v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: A key step in knowledge discovery is the evaluation of data mining results. In several applications, including pattern mining, graph analysis, and others, this step includes the evaluation of the statistical significance of the results, to avoid spuri...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> analysis, arxiv, few-shot, paper | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: PermDoRA -- Understanding Adapter Interference in Language Models: Limits of Parameter-Space Geometry
item.description: arXiv:2606.11262v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Access control in large language models (LLMs) requires modular mechanisms to enable domain-specific behavior without retraining or cross-domain interference. A common hypothesis is that interference during adapter composition arises from overlap in l...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> analysis, arxiv, framework, alignment, large language model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Shopping Reasoning Bench: An Expert-Authored Benchmark for Multi-Turn Conversational Shopping Assistants
item.description: arXiv:2606.12608v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Conversational shopping assistants now serve hundreds of millions of customers, yet no existing benchmark jointly evaluates the open-ended multi-turn reasoning, domain expertise, and criterion-level quality that real shopping conversations demand. Sho...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> analysis, product, GPT, reasoning, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Agent-based models for the evolution of morphological alternation patterns
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Dual-Stance Evaluation of Sycophancy: The Structure of Agreement and the Limits of Intervention
item.description: arXiv:2606.11205v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Activation steering can shift LLM behaviour, but standard evaluations do not typically test whether a sycophancy-reduction direction also suppresses agreement with factually correct statements. We introduce dual-stance evaluation, which tests both sta...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, analysis, LLM, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: ProHiFlo: Hierarchical Flow Matching with Functional Guidance for De Novo Protein Generation
item.description: arXiv:2606.11243v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: De novo protein generation has transformative potential in therapeutic design, enzyme engineering, and synthetic biology. While diffusion-based and flow matching approaches have achieved progress, they typically operate at single resolution and lack m...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, framework, model, RAG, experiment | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Physics-informed generative AI for semiconductor manufacturing: Enforcing hard physical constraints in generative models by construction
item.description: arXiv:2606.11247v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Generative models are increasingly used to propose designs, data, and control actions for physical systems, yet many such systems are governed by hard physical constraints rather than by perceptual plausibility. Semiconductor manufacturing provides a ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> multimodal, research, tool, model, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Helping Figures Tell their Story! Paper-Grounded Video Generation Explaining Complex Scientific Figures
item.description: arXiv:2606.12576v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Scientific figures compress complex pipelines into a single canvas, yet understanding them requires paper-grounded, step-by-step narration aligned with visual highlights a capability missing from current video generation systems and benchmarks. To add...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> multimodal, release, step-by-step, paper, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 40%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: From AGI to ASI
item.description: arXiv:2606.12683v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Over the last decade, building human-level artificial general intelligence has moved from far-fetched speculation to being a concrete next-decade target for many of the largest AI organisations. Achieving this goal would have profound and far-reaching...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> image, arxiv, research | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 40%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)
